title: Firebase setup: install SDK and create config/Firestore utilities (#154 pt 1)
body:
Part of #154

SCOPE: Install the Firebase JS SDK, create firebaseConfig.ts with Firestore initialization, and define the Firestore data schema that mirrors the existing IndexedDB data model (feeds, diapers, sleep, growth, pumping entries).

FILES:
- src/utils/firebaseConfig.ts (new) — Firebase app init, Firestore instance export
- src/utils/firestoreUtils.ts (new) — typed read/write helpers for each collection
- package.json — add firebase dependency

DEPENDENCIES: npm install firebase

NOTES: Use anonymous auth or no auth initially (user can manually add credentials). Export a single db instance. Do NOT wire into the app yet — just create the utilities.

TESTING: Unit tests for firestoreUtils serialization/deserialization with mocked Firestore.
enhanced_description:
Firebase Setup: Install SDK and create config/Firestore utilities (Part 1 of #154 Firebase autosync feature)

What was requested:
- Install the Firebase JS SDK (npm install firebase)
- Create firebaseConfig.ts: initialize Firebase app, export Firestore db instance
- Create firestoreUtils.ts: typed read/write helpers for each Firestore collection mirroring the IndexedDB data model (feeds, diapers, sleep, growth, pumping entries)
- Define the Firestore data schema consistent with the existing IndexedDB models

Affected components:
- src/utils/firebaseConfig.ts (NEW) — Firebase app init, Firestore instance export
- src/utils/firestoreUtils.ts (NEW) — typed CRUD helpers per collection
- package.json — add firebase dependency

Expected behavior:
- A db Firestore instance is exported from firebaseConfig.ts
- firestoreUtils.ts provides typed helpers like saveFeedEntry(), getFeedEntries(), etc. for each data type
- Uses anonymous auth or no auth initially (user provides credentials later via Settings UI in pt 3)
- Does NOT wire into existing app components — pure utility layer only

Current behavior:
- No Firebase integration exists; all data is stored only in IndexedDB locally

Edge cases to handle:
- Handle missing/invalid Firebase config gracefully (should not crash the app)
- Ensure Firestore data types match IndexedDB schema exactly to enable lossless sync later
- Document any required manual steps (Firebase project creation, credentials) in code comments

Acceptance criteria:
- firebase package appears in package.json dependencies
- firebaseConfig.ts exports a valid Firestore db instance (or a no-op stub when unconfigured)
- firestoreUtils.ts exports typed helpers for all tracked data types
- No existing app behavior is changed

Testing:
- Unit tests for firestoreUtils serialization/deserialization using mocked Firestore (vi.mock firebase/firestore)
- Test that each helper correctly maps IndexedDB entry shape to Firestore document shape and back
- Test graceful no-op behavior when Firebase is not yet configured
implementation_notes:
WHAT YOU ACTUALLY DID:
- Files changed:
  - src/utils/firebaseConfig.ts (NEW): Firebase app init and Firestore db instance management. Exports isValidFirestoreConfig(), initFirebaseConfig(), getFirestoreDb(), resetFirebaseConfig(). Uses lazy singleton pattern — safe to call multiple times. Returns null when unconfigured.
  - src/utils/firestoreUtils.ts (NEW): Typed CRUD helpers for all log categories. Exports toFirestoreDoc() and fromFirestoreDoc() as pure testable functions, plus saveEntry(), saveEntries() (batch), getEntries(), deleteEntry() generic helpers, and category-specific wrappers (getFeedEntries, getDiaperEntries, etc. for all 9 categories). All helpers are no-ops when db is null.
  - package.json: firebase ^12.11.0 already present — no change needed.
- Tests added:
  - tests/unit/firestoreUtils.test.ts: 35 tests covering isValidFirestoreConfig edge cases, firebaseConfig lifecycle (init/get/reset), toFirestoreDoc serialization (strips undefined, keeps null), fromFirestoreDoc round-trip, getEntries with mocked getDocs, saveEntry calling setDoc correctly, saveEntries batch behavior, deleteEntry, and category-specific helpers.
- Approach taken:
  - Uses Firestore (not RTDB) as specified in the issue
  - Firestore path: profiles/{profileId}/{category}/{entryId}
  - All async helpers accept db: Firestore | null — graceful no-op when unconfigured
  - Pure serialization helpers (toFirestoreDoc/fromFirestoreDoc) are exported for unit testing
  - Does NOT wire into existing app components — standalone utility layer only
- Trade-offs:
  - Existing RTDB sync (features/sync/) uses Firebase Realtime Database; this adds Firestore as a separate utility layer per the issue spec
  - firebase/app and firebase/firestore are statically imported (not dynamic) since this is a utility module, not a React hook

title: Firebase sync: core IndexedDB ↔ Firestore bidirectional sync service (#154 pt 2)
body:
Part of #154

SCOPE: Implement the bidirectional sync logic that reads from IndexedDB and writes to Firestore, and vice versa. Expose a useSyncHook hook that components can use to trigger/observe sync state.

FILES:
- src/hooks/useFirebaseSync.ts (new) — hook: syncAll(), syncStatus, lastSyncedAt
- src/utils/syncService.ts (new) — core merge logic (last-write-wins by timestamp)

DEPENDENCIES: Depends on sub-issue for Firebase utilities (firebaseConfig.ts, firestoreUtils.ts)

NOTES: Merge strategy = last-write-wins by  timestamp. Pull remote on app start, push local changes after each write. Handle offline gracefully (queue writes).

TESTING: Unit tests for merge conflict resolution, offline queue behavior.

title: Firebase sync: UI integration — sync status indicator and settings (#154 pt 3)
body:
Part of #154

SCOPE: Wire the sync hook into the app shell, show a sync status indicator, and add a Firebase credentials setup section in Settings so users can enter their own Firebase project config.

FILES:
- src/components/SyncStatusBadge.tsx (new) — small badge showing synced/syncing/error
- src/components/Settings.tsx (existing) — add Firebase config input fields
- src/App.tsx or main shell (existing) — call useFirebaseSync() on mount

DEPENDENCIES: Depends on sub-issues for Firebase utilities and sync service.

NOTES: Settings UI should accept apiKey, projectId, appId inputs and persist them to localStorage. Show a "Copy setup instructions" helper. The sync hook reads credentials from localStorage before initializing Firebase.

TESTING: Unit test that SyncStatusBadge renders correct state for each sync status value. Integration test for settings persistence.
